answers.body:
I worked it out implementing custom SpringLiquibase and SpringResourceAcessor and moving from liquibase 4.0 to 4.6.1

If anyone is interested, here's my code :

public class CustomSpringResourceAcessor extends SpringResourceAccessor {


    public CustomSpringResourceAcessor(ResourceLoader resourceLoader) {
        super(resourceLoader);
    }

    @Override
    protected String finalizeSearchPath(String searchPath) {
        return super.finalizeSearchPath(searchPath).substring(11);
    }

    @Override
    public InputStreamList openStreams(String relativeTo, String streamPath) throws IOException {
        String path = this.getClass().getProtectionDomain().getCodeSource().getLocation().getPath();
        path = path.substring(0, path.indexOf("/target"));

        if (relativeTo == null) {
            return super.openStreams(path, streamPath);
        }

        return super.openStreams(path + "/" + relativeTo, streamPath);
    }
}

and

public class CustomSpringLiquibase extends SpringLiquibase {
    @Override
    protected SpringResourceAccessor createResourceOpener() {
        return new CustomSpringResourceAcessor(getResourceLoader());
    }
}

Problem

I faced the same. When tried to set absolute path to change-log file, Liquibase throwed error:

“Specifying files by absolute path was removed in Liquibase 4.0. Please use a relative path or add ‘/’ to the classpath parameter.”

Solution

Create Liquibase configuration which supports Path resources and de-sanitize URIs
Hope it will help somebody 😇

  @TestConfiguration
  class Config {
    private static final String ROOT_CHANGELOG_FILENAME = "db.changelog-root.xml";

    @Bean
    @Primary
    public SpringLiquibase enableDatabaseTestLiquibase(DataSource dataSource) {
      Path migrationRootFolderPath = Paths.get("%Absolute path to ROOT_CHANGELOG_FILENAME  file%");
      Path changeLogFilePath = migrationRootFolderPath.resolve(ROOT_CHANGELOG_FILENAME);

      SpringLiquibase liquibase = new AbsPathLiquibase(migrationRootFolderPath);
      liquibase.setContexts(DEFAULT_PROFILE);
      liquibase.setResourceLoader(new FileSystemResourceLoader());
      liquibase.setChangeLog(changeLogFilePath.toString());
      liquibase.setDataSource(dataSource);
      liquibase.setShouldRun(true);
      return liquibase;
    }

    public static class AbsPathLiquibase extends SpringLiquibase {
      private final SpringResourceAccessor resourceAccessor;

      public AbsPathLiquibase(Path migrationRootFolderAbsPath) {
        super();
        this.resourceAccessor =
            new AbsPathResourceAccessor(getResourceLoader(), migrationRootFolderAbsPath);
      }

      @Override
      protected SpringResourceAccessor createResourceOpener() {
        return resourceAccessor;
      }
    }

    
    public static class AbsPathResourceAccessor extends SpringResourceAccessor {
      private final Path migrationRootFolderAbsPath;

      public AbsPathResourceAccessor(
          ResourceLoader resourceLoader, Path migrationRootFolderAbsPath) {
        super(resourceLoader);
        this.migrationRootFolderAbsPath = migrationRootFolderAbsPath;
      }

      @Override
      public List<Resource> getAll(String path) throws IOException {
        Path resolvedAbsPath = resolveAbsPathIfExists(path);
        if (resolvedAbsPath == null) {
          String absPathStr = fromSanitizedToAbsPath(path);
          resolvedAbsPath = resolveAbsPathIfExists(absPathStr);
        }

        if (resolvedAbsPath != null) {
          return List.of(new PathResource(path, resolvedAbsPath));
        }

        return List.of();
      }

      private @Nullable Path resolveAbsPathIfExists(String path) {
        Path pathObj = Paths.get(path);
        if (pathObj.isAbsolute() && Files.exists(pathObj)) {
          return pathObj;
        }
        return null;
      }

      /**
       * Converts a sanitized path to an absolute path by resolving it against the migration root
       * folder. If the path contains the migration root folder name, it will extract the suffix
       * after that folder and resolve it against the absolute migration root path.
       *
       * @param sanitizedPath the path "sanitized" on previous steps by {@link
       *     DatabaseChangeLog#normalizePath(String)}
       * @return the absolute path
       */
      private String fromSanitizedToAbsPath(String sanitizedPath) {
        String migrationRootFolderName = migrationRootFolderAbsPath.getFileName().toString();

        int migrationRootFolderIndex = sanitizedPath.lastIndexOf(migrationRootFolderName);
        if (migrationRootFolderIndex >= 0) {
          // Extract the portion after the root folder name
          int pathSuffixStart = migrationRootFolderIndex + migrationRootFolderName.length();
          String pathSuffix = sanitizedPath.substring(pathSuffixStart);

          // Remove leading slash if present
          if (pathSuffix.startsWith("/") || pathSuffix.startsWith("\\")) {
            pathSuffix = pathSuffix.substring(1);
          }

          // Resolve suffix against the root path if not empty
          return pathSuffix.isEmpty()
              ? migrationRootFolderAbsPath.toAbsolutePath().toString()
              : migrationRootFolderAbsPath.resolve(pathSuffix).toAbsolutePath().toString();
        }

        // If the path doesn't contain the migration root folder name, just resolve it directly
        return migrationRootFolderAbsPath.resolve(sanitizedPath).toString();
      }
    }
  }

title: SpringLiquibase with Liquibase 4 and FileSystemResourceLoader
body:
I'm currently facing a problem. My project looks like this :
Project
|_ module 1
   |_ liquibase
      |_ migration.xml
      |_ file1.xml
   |_ src
      |_ main
         |_ java
         |_ resources

To be able to launch component tests, I run, using docker, a postgresql container. I want to launch my liquibase scripts.

Here's a my code :

        SpringLiquibase liquibase = new SpringLiquibase();
        liquibase.setResourceLoader(new FileSystemResourceLoader());
        liquibase.setDataSource(dataSource);
        liquibase.setChangeLog("liquibase/migration.xml");
        liquibase.setDefaultSchema("mySchema");
        liquibase.setDropFirst(false);
        liquibase.setShouldRun(true);
        try {
            liquibase.afterPropertiesSet();
            log.info("Liquibase run ended");
        } catch (Exception e) {
            log.error(e.getMessage());
            throw new RuntimeException(e.getMessage());
        }

This has run well for a long time, until I made an update to Liquibase 4. Now, I'm getting the following error : Specifying files by absolute path was removed in Liquibase 4.0. Please use a relative path or add '/' to the classpath parameter.

I searched throught the web and didn't find anything helpful. I tried a lot of different things, and nothing worked

Someone has a clue ? (other than moving my liquibase folder inside resources)
